functions.label: createPublicStepDefinition
description:
Helper function to create a PublicStepDefinition with automatic type inference.
This ensures that the editorHandlers' types are correctly inferred
from the inputSchema and configSchema without needing explicit type annotations.

interfaces.label: PublicStepDefinition
description:
User-facing metadata for a workflow step.
This is used by the UI to display step information (label, description, icon, schemas, documentation).

children.label: icon
description:
Icon type from EUI icon library.
Used to visually represent this step type in the UI.
kibana icon will be used if not provided
TODO: add support for EuiIconType

children.label: editorHandlers
description: Property handlers for the step.

interfaces.label: PublicTriggerDefinition
description:
User-facing definition for a workflow trigger.
Used by the UI to display trigger information (title, description, icon, event schema, documentation).
Extends the server contract (id + eventSchema) with UI-only fields.

children.label: title
description:
Short human-readable name for this trigger.
Displayed in the UI when selecting or viewing triggers.

children.label: description
description:
User-facing description of when this trigger is emitted.
Displayed as help text or in tooltips.

children.label: icon
description: Used to visually represent this trigger in the UI.

children.label: documentation
description: Documentation (details + examples), aligned with step definitions.

children.label: snippets
description: Pre-filled values for snippet insertion (e.g. on.condition).

interfaces.label: StepDocumentation
description: Documentation information for a workflow step.

children.label: details
description: Detailed description with usage examples (markdown supported)

children.label: url
description: External documentation URL

children.label: examples
description: Usage examples in YAML format

interfaces.label: TriggerDocumentation
description: Documentation for a trigger (aligned with steps: details + generic examples).

children.label: details
description: Detailed description with usage examples (markdown supported)

children.label: examples
description:
Usage examples as markdown strings (e.g. "## Title\n```yaml\n...").
Shown in the YAML editor hover.

interfaces.label: TriggerSnippets
description: Pre-filled snippet values when the user adds this trigger from the UI.

children.label: condition
description:
KQL condition pre-filled in the trigger's `on.condition` when the user adds this
trigger from the UI (actions menu or YAML autocomplete).
Must be valid KQL and only reference properties from the event schema (validated at registration).

setup.label: WorkflowsExtensionsPublicPluginSetup
description:
Public-side plugin setup contract.
Exposes methods for other plugins to register public-side step and trigger definitions.

children.label: registerStepDefinition
description:
Register user-facing definition for a workflow step.
This should be called during the plugin's setup phase.

children.label: definition
description: - The public-side step definition, or a function that returns a promise of the definition (e.g. for dynamic imports)

children.label: registerTriggerDefinition
description:
Register user-facing definition for a workflow trigger.
This should be called during the plugin's setup phase.
Must be paired with server-side registration (registerTrigger) for the same trigger id.

children.label: definition
description: - The public-side trigger definition

start.label: WorkflowsExtensionsPublicPluginStart
description:
Public-side plugin start contract.
Exposes methods for retrieving registered step and trigger definitions.

classes.label: TriggerRegistry
description: Registry for workflow trigger definitions.

children.label: register
description:
Register a trigger definition.
Must be called during plugin setup. Validates id format and eventSchema (Zod object).

children.label: definition
description: - The server trigger definition (id + eventSchema)

children.label: freeze
description:
Freeze the registry so no further registrations are allowed.
Called when the plugin starts; after this, only get/has/list are valid.

children.label: get
description: Get a trigger definition by id.

children.label: triggerId
description: - The trigger identifier

returnComment: The trigger definition, or undefined if not found

children.label: has
description: Check if a trigger is registered.

children.label: triggerId
description: - The trigger identifier

returnComment: True if the trigger is registered, false otherwise

children.label: list
description: Get all registered trigger definitions.

functions.label: createServerStepDefinition
description:
Helper function to create a ServerStepDefinition with automatic type inference.
This ensures that the handler's input and output types are correctly inferred
from the inputSchema and outputSchema without needing explicit type annotations.

functions.label: getEventChainContext
description:
Returns the event-chain context for the request.

Two trusted paths:
 1. In-process (fakeRequest): the execution engine called setWorkflowEventChainContext before
    any step runs, so the Symbol is present and takes precedence.
 2. Inbound HTTP from kibana.request step: no Symbol exists, but the step marks its outbound
    call with x-elastic-internal-origin so request.isInternalApiRequest is true. Only then are
    the event-chain headers parsed.

Note: isInternalApiRequest is derived from the presence of the x-elastic-internal-origin request
header (see KibanaRequest constructor). It is not enforced at the network layer, so a
sufficiently informed external caller could set it. This gate stops naive spoofing but should
not be treated as a hard trust boundary.

functions.label: getOutboundEventChainHeaders
description:
Returns headers that should be added to outbound HTTP requests to Kibana
when the request is made from a workflow step (e.g. kibana.request).
Enables the server to restore event-chain context and enforce depth limits.

functions.label: setWorkflowEventChainContext
description:
Sets the event-chain context on the request. Called by the execution engine
at the start of a workflow run so that any emitEvent call using this request
(e.g. in-process with the fakeRequest) will have the correct depth and sourceExecutionId.

interfaces.label: EmitEventParams
description: Parameters for emitEvent.

interfaces.label: EventChainContext
description:
Context attached to the request when a workflow runs, so that when code
in that workflow emits an event (e.g. via emitEvent), we can infer the
event-chain depth and enforce a cap to prevent infinite loops.
Depth is incremented for every step in the chain (any workflow); the trigger
event handler caps scheduling when depth exceeds the configured max.

interfaces.label: ServerStepDefinition
description:
Definition of a server-side workflow step extension.
Contains the technical/behavioral implementation of a step.

The input and output types are automatically inferred from the inputSchema and outputSchema,
so you don't need to specify them explicitly. Use `createServerStepDefinition` helper function
for the best type inference experience.

children.label: handler
description:
The handler function that executes this step's logic.
Input and output types are automatically inferred from the schemas.

children.label: onCancel
description:
Optional cancellation cleanup handler.

Called after the step's abort signal fires and `run()` completes, giving the
step a guaranteed cleanup entry point (e.g. cancelling spawned child operations).
Only invoked when the workflow is being cancelled — normal completions skip it.

Implementations must be idempotent. Errors thrown here are logged but do not
disrupt the cancellation flow.

interfaces.label: StepHandlerContext
description:
Context provided to custom step handlers during execution.
This gives access to runtime services needed for step execution.

children.label: input
description: The validated input provided to the step based on inputSchema
signature: TInput extends { _zod: { output: any; }; } ? TInput["_zod"]["output"] : unknown

children.label: config
description: The config provided to the step based on configSchema
signature: TConfig extends { _zod: { output: any; }; } ? TConfig["_zod"]["output"] : unknown

children.label: rawInput
description:
The raw input configuration before template rendering.
Has the same shape as input, but values may contain template strings.

children.label: contextManager
description: Runtime context manager for accessing workflow state, context, and template evaluation

children.label: logger
description: Logger scoped to this step execution
signature: { debug(message: string, meta?: object | undefined): void; info(message: string, meta?: object | undefined): void; warn(message: string, meta?: object | undefined): void; error(message: string, error?: Error | undefined): void; }

children.label: abortSignal
description: Abort signal for cancellation support

children.label: stepId
description: Current step's ID

children.label: stepType
description: Current step's type

interfaces.label: StepHandlerResult
description: Result returned by a custom step handler

children.label: output
description:
Output data from the step execution
This will be available to subsequent steps via template expressions

children.label: error
description: Optional error information if the step failed

interfaces.label: TriggerEventHandlerParams
description: Parameters passed to the trigger event handler when an event is emitted.

interfaces.label: WorkflowsClient
description:
Request-scoped client for emitting workflow trigger events.
Use from route handlers via ctx.workflows.getWorkflowsClient().

children.label: emitEvent
description: Emit an event for the given trigger. Subscribed workflows in the current request's space will run.

children.label: triggerId
description: - Must match a trigger registered via registerTriggerDefinition

children.label: payload
description: - Event payload; available in workflows as context.event

interfaces.label: WorkflowsExtensionsServerPluginSetupDeps
description: Dependencies for the server plugin setup phase.

interfaces.label: WorkflowsExtensionsServerPluginStartDeps
description: Dependencies for the server plugin start phase.

interfaces.label: WorkflowsRouteHandlerContext
description: Workflows route handler context. Available as ctx.workflows for plugins that depend on workflows_extensions.

children.label: getWorkflowsClient
description: Returns a request-scoped client bound to the current request and space.

misc.label: EmitEventFn
description: Emits a trigger event. Validates trigger id, then invokes the registered handler (which logs and runs subscribed workflows).

misc.label: EVENT_CHAIN_DEPTH_HEADER
description:
HTTP header name used when a workflow step (e.g. kibana.request) makes an outbound request
to Kibana. The execution engine sets this to the current event-chain depth so the server
can restore context on the incoming request and enforce the event-chain depth cap.

misc.label: EVENT_CHAIN_SOURCE_EXECUTION_HEADER
description:
HTTP header for the workflow execution id associated with the current chain hop. Set on outbound
requests so the server can restore `sourceExecutionId` on the incoming request.

misc.label: OnCancelHandler
description:
Cancellation cleanup handler for custom workflow steps.
Receives the same context as the main handler so it has access to input,
config, and runtime services needed for cleanup.

misc.label: ServerTriggerDefinition
description: Server-side alias: same as CommonTriggerDefinition (used when registering on the server).

misc.label: StepHandler
description:
Handler function that executes a custom workflow step.
This function is called by the execution engine to run the step logic.
returnComment: The step output (should conform to outputSchema)

children.label: context
description: - The runtime context for the step execution

misc.label: WorkflowExecutionFailedEvent
signature: { workflow: { id: string; name: string; spaceId: string; isErrorHandler: boolean; }; execution: { id: string; startedAt: string; failedAt: string; }; error: { message: string; stepId?: string | undefined; stepName?: string | undefined; stepExecutionId?: string | undefined; }; }

setup.label: WorkflowsExtensionsServerPluginSetup
description:
Server-side plugin setup contract.
Exposes methods for other plugins to register server-side custom workflow steps and triggers.

children.label: registerStepDefinition
description:
Register server-side definition for a workflow step.
This should be called during the plugin's setup phase.

children.label: definition
description: - The step server-side definition

children.label: registerTriggerDefinition
description:
Register a workflow trigger definition.
This should be called during the plugin's setup phase.

children.label: definition
description: - The trigger definition

children.label: registerTriggerEventHandler
description:
Register the handler invoked when emitEvent is called.
Should be called during the plugin's setup phase.

children.label: handler
description: - Function called with triggerId, spaceId, and payload when an event is emitted

start.label: WorkflowsExtensionsServerPluginStart
description:
Server-side plugin start contract.
Exposes step definitions (from common contract), trigger definitions, and emitEvent.

interfaces.label: CommonTriggerDefinition
description:
Shared trigger contract (common to server and public).

Constraints (enforced at registration):
- id: globally unique, namespaced format <solution>.<event>
- eventSchema: must be a Zod object schema that rejects unknown fields

children.label: id
description: Globally unique, namespaced identifier (e.g. cases.updated, alerts.severity_high)

children.label: eventSchema
description:
Payload contract (Zod object schema; must reject unknown fields by default).
Adding descriptions to properties (e.g. with .describe()) is recommended so they will
help users to understand the data they will receive with the event.

misc.label: CommonStepDefinition
description:
Common step definition fields shared between server and public.
Extends BaseStepDefinition from @kbn/workflows with the same generic schema triple.
Input and output types are automatically inferred from the schemas.

misc.label: EVENT_FIELD_PREFIX
description:
Field prefix used in trigger condition KQL (e.g. "event.severity").
Use this when calling validateKqlAgainstSchema for trigger conditions so paths and error messages stay consistent.

misc.label: WorkflowExecutionFailedEvent
signature: { workflow: { id: string; name: string; spaceId: string; isErrorHandler: boolean; }; execution: { id: string; startedAt: string; failedAt: string; }; error: { message: string; stepId?: string | undefined; stepName?: string | undefined; stepExecutionId?: string | undefined; }; }
